name: project-baseline
description: Testable readiness standard. v1's 20 rules (build/test/context/claims) distilled from three of the author's own repo reviews, plus v2 additions pressure-tested against the prior-art field (OpenSSF Scorecard, SLSA, 12-factor, Google SRE, Diataxis, repolinter, Backstage/Cortex/OpsLevel, and ~40 more). Each rule maps to a lesson or a cited source; blockers fail CI, manual rules resolve via a dated sign-off ledger. Rules with a 'profile' only run when that profile is active: core is always on; 'service' auto-activates when project_type=service; 'advanced' is opt-in via config.profiles.

profiles.core: Always on. Universal, high-confidence, machine-checkable rules.
profiles.service: Auto-on when project_type=service (or listed in config.profiles). Operability/observability rules that only make sense for a running service.
profiles.advanced: Opt-in via config.profiles. Expert/niche rules (SBOM, code-scanning, mutation testing, symbol-integrity) that would be noise on most repos.

rules.title: Dependency manifest present
rules.lesson: SW-1 build-readiness is proven by execution
rules.rationale: No manifest = nothing to install = Task 1 can't run.
rules.fix: Add package.json / pyproject.toml / go.mod / Cargo.toml as Task 0.

rules.title: Lockfile committed
rules.rationale: Reproducible installs; a clean checkout resolves the same deps.
rules.fix: Commit pnpm-lock.yaml / poetry.lock / uv.lock / Cargo.lock / go.sum.

rules.title: CI workflow present
rules.lesson: SW-1 / CTX-7 make a robot check it
rules.rationale: Enforcement lives in CI, not in a human remembering to run checks.
rules.fix: Add .github/workflows/ci.yml (or equivalent) that lints, typechecks, tests.

rules.title: Env/secret template present
rules.rationale: A joining dev can boot without guessing env vars; keeps secrets out of code.
rules.fix: Add .env.example enumerating every required var (no real values).

rules.title: Task 1 passes on a clean checkout
rules.rationale: 'Ready to build' = a fresh clone can install and run the first test (green or intentionally red). Skipped by --no-exec.
rules.fix: Set config.bootstrap_command to your install+test command; make it pass on a clean tree.

rules.title: Baseline gate wired into CI
rules.lesson: CTX-7 extend enforcement to the narrative layer
rules.rationale: This standard only bites if CI runs it on every PR.
rules.fix: Add a CI step: node tools/baseline/check.mjs.

rules.title: A single documented bootstrap entrypoint exists
rules.rationale: BUILD-05 proves the bootstrap works; this proves a newcomer can FIND it without tribal knowledge — one conventional command, not a wiki page.
rules.fix: Add bin/setup (or a Makefile/Taskfile 'setup' target) and reference it by name in the README.

rules.title: Standard task commands are declared machine-readably
rules.rationale: A 'test' and 'lint' command declared in the manifest (not only prose) lets CI, tooling, and newcomers invoke them the same way.
rules.fix: Add test + lint scripts to package.json (or test:/lint: targets to Makefile/Taskfile, or [tool] entries to pyproject).

rules.title: Bootstrap is idempotent (safe to re-run)
rules.rationale: A setup script that clobbers or hard-fails on a second run breaks the 'just run setup' promise the moment anyone re-runs it.
rules.fix: Make the bootstrap command exit 0 when invoked twice on an already-set-up checkout (guard destructive steps).

rules.title: Automated tests exist
rules.rationale: Acceptance criteria backed by nothing are prose.
rules.fix: Add at least one real test under test/ or *.test.*/*_test.py.

rules.title: Failure paths are tested (negative tests)
rules.lesson: SW-2 a test must try to break the promise
rules.rationale: A test that never exercises the failing input passes without proving anything.
rules.fix: For every 'must raise / must reject / must 4xx', add a test asserting it (toThrow / rejects / pytest.raises).

rules.title: Red-on-arrival guards for 'must-never-exist' invariants
rules.rationale: An invariant about something that must NOT exist is only real if a pinned test (e.g. a table/enum count) goes red when it appears.
rules.fix: Add a count/allow-list assertion for each 'must never appear' rule, then record it in the sign-off ledger.

rules.title: Acceptance criteria reconciled against reference code
rules.lesson: SW-2 criteria vs their own example code
rules.rationale: Specs whose acceptance criteria contradict their own reference impl create wrong-either-way forks.
rules.fix: Before 'ready', confirm every enum/field named in a criterion exists in its type def; record sign-off.

rules.title: Mutation testing, if used, is gated
rules.lesson: SW-2 tests must be able to fail
rules.rationale: A mutation run that never fails the build is decoration; a break-threshold >0 turns a weak suite red.
rules.fix: In stryker.conf.* set thresholds.break to a value >0; or run mutmut in --CI mode with a floor.

rules.title: Flaky-test quarantine is disciplined
rules.rationale: A growing pile of skipped tests silently erodes coverage unless each skip carries an owner+reason+deadline and the total stays under a cap.
rules.fix: Give every quarantined test an owner+deadline (marker or central register) and cap the total; record sign-off.

rules.title: Status lives in one owner with a fresh 'last-verified' stamp
rules.lesson: CTX-6 status is a computed exit-state
rules.rationale: The costliest context failure is misdirecting the next session into redoing settled work.
rules.fix: Add 'last-verified: <short-sha> <date>' to the status file; reconcile it whenever you update the status doc.

rules.title: Every decision record carries a Status; superseded ones link forward
rules.lesson: CTX-5 no LOCKED artifact without a forward supersede-link
rules.rationale: A frozen decision the repo has overtaken, with no in-file 'Superseded by', actively misdirects a reader.
rules.fix: Give each ADR a Status: field; when a decision reverses, flip the old one to Superseded and add 'Superseded by <id>' in the same commit.

rules.title: Sources of truth are declared
rules.rationale: Load-bearing values copied into N docs drift to M values; declaring owners is step one.
rules.fix: Populate config.sources_of_truth (e.g. status, facts, decisions) so each fact class has one home.

rules.title: No frozen/consolidated doc without regeneration or supersede banners
rules.rationale: A 'consolidated' spec is only safe if regenerated from its sources or stamped with in-file supersede banners.
rules.fix: Regenerate consolidated specs from their ADRs, or add supersede-aware banners; record sign-off.

rules.title: No broken internal doc links
rules.lesson: CTX doc-drift: a dead link is provable drift
rules.rationale: A relative link to a doc/file that no longer exists is the most common, most mechanical form of doc rot — and it is deterministically checkable.
rules.fix: Fix or remove the broken links the check reports; wire the check into CI so new ones can't land.

rules.title: Long-lived docs carry a freshness contract
rules.lesson: CTX-6 freshness as a computed state
rules.rationale: A doc with no 'last reviewed' date can rot invisibly; a per-doc TTL turns silent staleness into a red check.
rules.fix: Add 'last_review_date' (or ttl_days) to the frontmatter of docs under config.freshness_globs; refresh on review. Opt-in: empty freshness_globs skips this rule.

rules.title: Superseded ADRs link forward to a file that exists
rules.rationale: CTX-02 checks a superseded ADR HAS a forward link; this checks the link actually resolves to a present replacement file.
rules.fix: When you mark an ADR 'Superseded by NNNN', ensure a decision file numbered NNNN exists.

rules.title: Generated files carry a 'DO NOT EDIT' provenance marker
rules.rationale: A generated file with no marker gets hand-edited and silently diverges from its source; the marker makes provenance machine-visible.
rules.fix: Prepend a 'Code generated ... DO NOT EDIT.' header to generated files; list their globs in config.generated_globs. Opt-in: empty generated_globs skips this rule.

check.pattern: Code generated .*DO NOT EDIT|@generated|DO NOT EDIT

rules.title: Required grounding docs exist and are non-empty
rules.lesson: CTX-4 grounding docs have one home
rules.rationale: The architecture/tech-stack docs a new session relies on are only load-bearing if they exist and are non-empty at their declared home.
rules.fix: List the grounding docs you rely on in config.grounding_docs; create any that are missing/empty. Opt-in: empty grounding_docs skips this rule.

rules.title: Code symbols/paths named in docs still resolve
rules.rationale: A doc that cites a file path in backticks which no longer exists is silent drift; path-integrity catches the mechanical half.
rules.fix: Fix or update the backticked file paths the check reports as missing.

rules.title: A claims register exists
rules.rationale: Competitive/novelty/security claims need one structured, checkable home — not scattered README prose.
rules.fix: Add the claims register (see templates/CLAIMS.json); set makes_external_claims:false to opt out.

rules.title: Every claim tagged with a build-state
rules.lesson: Claims-4 future tense for unbuilt work
rules.rationale: Design-only mechanisms narrated as shipped fact mislead readers (esp. security).
rules.fix: Give each claim build_state ∈ shipped-tested | designed-unshipped | assumed-unmeasured.

rules.title: Every claim graded by blast radius
rules.lesson: Claims-1 grade by blast radius not confidence
rules.rationale: A false claim that IS the wedge is existential; it needs a higher bar + a de-risking milestone before code.
rules.fix: Add blast_radius ∈ existential | recoverable; existential claims must have a de_risk_milestone.

rules.title: Novelty/competitive claims have a dated prior-art pass
rules.rationale: 'Nobody does this' is a decaying search result; one competitor grep can falsify a whole thesis.
rules.fix: For each novelty/competitive/market claim add prior_art_checked:<date> and how_we_differ; re-check on cadence.

rules.title: Citations resolve and support the claim
rules.lesson: Claims-3 cite the paper that proves your point
rules.rationale: A real citation on the wrong claim launders an assumption into a 'fact'.
rules.fix: Each citation needs a url and a one-line supports_because; confirm the source's conclusion supports you.

rules.title: Wedge and moat are stated and pressure-tested
rules.rationale: The hard-to-build part (interception, a pipeline) is often already commoditized; the moat must survive assuming it's free.
rules.fix: Declare wedge + moat explicitly; pressure-test the moat assuming the hard mechanism is free; record sign-off.

rules.title: Specs of record carry explicit acceptance criteria
rules.lesson: Definition-of-Ready: no work item without acceptance criteria
rules.rationale: A spec with no acceptance criteria section is unfalsifiable — nothing can later prove it was met.
rules.fix: Add a non-empty 'Acceptance Criteria' section to each spec (or an acceptance_criteria field per register item).

rules.title: No high-signal secrets committed
rules.lesson: 12-factor: a repo could go open-source at any moment
rules.rationale: A committed private key or cloud credential is an immediate breach and survives in git history.
rules.fix: Remove the secret, rotate it, and add a secret-scanner (gitleaks/trufflehog) to CI.

rules.title: Real .env files are git-ignored, not committed
rules.lesson: 12-factor III: config in the environment, not the repo
rules.rationale: A committed .env leaks live secrets; only the template (.env.example) belongs in the tree.
rules.fix: git rm --cached the .env file, add '.env' to .gitignore, rotate anything exposed.

rules.title: Third-party CI actions pinned to a commit SHA
rules.rationale: A mutable action ref (@v4, @main) lets an upstream tag move under you and run arbitrary code in a token-bearing runner.
rules.fix: Pin every external 'uses:' to a full 40-char commit SHA (append a # v4 comment for readability).

rules.title: No dangerous CI workflow patterns
rules.rationale: Interpolating untrusted event text into a run: script, or checking out PR-head under pull_request_target, are the two canonical CI RCE paths. Heuristic — treat hits as review flags.
rules.fix: Move untrusted input into an env: var and reference it as "$VAR"; never checkout the PR head under pull_request_target with write scope.

rules.title: Automated dependency-update tool configured
rules.rationale: A committed lockfile freezes deps at a point in time; without an update bot those pins silently rot into known-CVE territory.
rules.fix: Add .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json; or wire an SCA step (npm audit / pip-audit / osv-scanner) into CI.

rules.title: Security policy names a reporting channel
rules.rationale: A SECURITY.md that exists but names no way to report a vuln is presence theater; the reporting channel is the load-bearing part.
rules.fix: Add SECURITY.md with a reporting email or a link to your GitHub security advisories, plus supported-versions info.

rules.title: No committed binary/executable artifacts
rules.rationale: A committed binary can't be reviewed and may hide malware or an unreproducible build; source should build the artifact, not ship it.
rules.fix: Remove the binary, build it from source in CI, and gitignore the output path (allow-list genuine test fixtures/vendor).

rules.title: A committed SBOM exists in a recognized format
rules.rationale: An SBOM lets downstream consumers answer 'am I affected by CVE-X' without cloning and building.
rules.fix: Generate and commit a CycloneDX (*.cdx.json) or SPDX (*.spdx.json) SBOM in CI.

rules.title: Static code-scanning is configured
rules.rationale: CodeQL/Semgrep in CI catches injection and unsafe-API classes that unit tests miss; it should fail the build, not merely annotate.
rules.fix: Add a CodeQL or Semgrep workflow and configure it to fail on findings (semgrep --error, or a required check-run).

rules.title: Release provenance/signing is present
rules.rationale: Signed provenance lets consumers verify an artifact was built by your pipeline, not swapped in transit.
rules.fix: Add actions/attest-build-provenance, slsa-github-generator, or cosign signing to your release workflow.

rules.title: Merge protection is declared in-repo
rules.rationale: Green CI means nothing if anyone can force-push to main; protection-as-code makes the merge gate reviewable. Skips if you configure protection in the GitHub UI (nothing in-repo to check).
rules.fix: Commit a ruleset (.github/rulesets/*.json), a probot settings.yml, or a terraform github_branch_protection that requires reviews and disables force-push.

rules.title: Strict/up-to-date merges and conversation resolution enabled
rules.rationale: Without strict status checks a stale branch can merge green; without conversation-resolution, unresolved review comments merge silently.
rules.fix: In the committed ruleset set strict status checks and required_conversation_resolution to true.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS exists and names an owner
rules.lesson: every path has an accountable reviewer
rules.rationale: A CODEOWNERS file routes reviews to the people accountable for each path, in one canonical location.
rules.fix: Add CODEOWNERS (or .github/CODEOWNERS) with at least one path→owner mapping.

rules.title: LICENSE file present
rules.rationale: No license = legally all-rights-reserved; nobody can safely use or contribute, and it's the most trivially checkable defect.
rules.fix: Add a LICENSE (or COPYING) file at the repo root; pick one at choosealicense.com.

rules.title: README exists with newcomer-critical sections
rules.rationale: A README that lacks what-it-is / install / usage forces newcomers to read code to get started.
rules.fix: Add a root README with install/setup and usage/quickstart sections.

rules.title: CHANGELOG present with an Unreleased section
rules.rationale: A changelog with an Unreleased section gives consumers a human-readable record of what changed and a place to accrue the next release's notes.
rules.fix: Add CHANGELOG.md following keepachangelog.com, including an '## [Unreleased]' section.

rules.title: A linter is configured
rules.rationale: Tests prove behavior; a linter catches the class of defects tests don't (unused vars, unsafe patterns) before review.
rules.fix: Add a linter config: eslint.config.js / .eslintrc, a [tool.ruff] section, .golangci.yml, .rubocop.yml, or biome.json.

rules.title: A formatter is configured
rules.rationale: A configured formatter ends style debates and keeps diffs about substance.
rules.fix: Add a formatter config: .prettierrc / prettier key, [tool.black] or [tool.ruff.format], rustfmt.toml (Go: gofmt is implied).

rules.title: Type-checking is strict where supported
rules.lesson: strict types catch a defect class at the door
rules.rationale: TS without strict, or mypy without strict, lets whole categories of null/any bugs through; the strict flag is the cheap on-switch.
rules.fix: Set compilerOptions.strict=true in tsconfig (or strict in mypy/pyright) and invoke the typecheck in CI.

rules.title: CI installs dependencies in frozen/locked mode
rules.rationale: A plain 'install' can silently resolve deps that drift from the lockfile; a frozen install fails when the lockfile is out of sync.
rules.fix: Use npm ci / pnpm install --frozen-lockfile / yarn --immutable / uv sync / poetry install (no update) in CI.

rules.title: Runtime version is pinned
rules.lesson: pin the toolchain, not just the libs
rules.rationale: Without a pinned runtime, two machines can build with different Node/Python majors and get different results.
rules.fix: Add .tool-versions / .nvmrc / .python-version / mise.toml, or an engines/requires-python/go directive.

rules.title: Pinned runtime version is consistent everywhere
rules.lesson: version single-home, no drift (serves CONTEXT too)
rules.rationale: When the runtime is PINNED in more than one home (.nvmrc, .tool-versions, Dockerfile FROM) those pins drift; a mismatch means 'works on my machine' bugs. Version RANGES (engines/requires-python) and CI test-matrices are not pins and are excluded.
rules.fix: Make the runtime major agree across .nvmrc/.tool-versions, CI setup-node/setup-python, Dockerfile FROM, and engines.

rules.title: Dockerfile base images pinned by digest
rules.rationale: FROM node:20 or :latest floats; the same Dockerfile builds different images over time. A digest pin makes the base reproducible.
rules.fix: Pin every FROM to name:tag@sha256:<digest>; skips if there's no Dockerfile.

rules.title: Structured logging is wired in
rules.rationale: Ad-hoc print/console logging can't be parsed, correlated, or leveled in prod; a structured logger is the minimum operability bar.
rules.fix: Adopt a structured logger (pino/winston, zap/zerolog, structlog/slog) writing to stdout.

rules.title: A health/readiness endpoint exists
rules.rationale: Without a health endpoint an orchestrator can't tell if the service is alive/ready, so it can't route or restart it correctly.
rules.fix: Add a /health (and /ready) route and point a liveness/readiness probe at it.

rules.title: Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM
rules.rationale: A service that ignores SIGTERM drops in-flight requests on every deploy/scale-down.
rules.fix: Handle SIGTERM: stop accepting new work, drain in-flight, close listeners, then exit.

rules.title: Outbound calls are time-bounded/guarded
rules.rationale: An unbounded call to a slow dependency cascades into resource exhaustion; timeouts + retry/breaker contain the blast. Heuristic.
rules.fix: Set explicit timeouts on HTTP/DB clients and add retry or circuit-breaker logic on critical paths.

rules.title: An operational runbook exists
rules.rationale: When it breaks at 3am, an on-call needs a rollback + escalation path, not to reverse-engineer the system live.
rules.fix: Add a runbook (runbooks/, docs/runbooks/, or RUNBOOK.md) covering rollback steps and an escalation/on-call contact.

rules.title: A service descriptor declares owner + lifecycle
rules.lesson: catalog: every service has an owner
rules.rationale: A parseable descriptor (owner + lifecycle/tier) is how a platform knows who to page and how critical the service is.
rules.fix: Add catalog-info.yaml (Backstage), opslevel.yml, or cortex.yaml with owner and lifecycle populated.

rules.title: CI actually invokes the test suite
rules.lesson: a CI job that never runs tests is theater
rules.rationale: BUILD-03 checks a CI file exists; this checks it actually runs tests — a cheap static complement to BUILD-05 (which only runs when you wire the bootstrap command).
rules.fix: Add a test step to CI: npm test / pytest / go test / make test / cargo test / gradle test / tox.

check.pattern: npm (run )?test|pnpm (run )?test|yarn test|node --test|deno test|bun test|pytest|python -m pytest|go test|make test|(?<![./\w])(jest|vitest|mocha)\b(?![-\w])|cargo test|\.?/?gradlew? test|mvn test|rspec|phpunit|dotnet test|\btox\b

rules.title: A coverage floor is declared and enforced
rules.lesson: a coverage regression should exit non-zero
rules.rationale: A coverage number nobody gates on drifts down; a fail-under threshold makes a regression fail the build.
rules.fix: Set a numeric floor: coverage.py fail_under, jest coverageThreshold, a codecov target, or --cov-fail-under in CI.

rules.title: Docs don't lag the code they anchor
rules.lesson: docs trailing code is the drift the lessons are about
rules.rationale: A doc that declares which source files it documents is stale if that code was committed well after the doc; git dates make this checkable. Opt-in: only docs with a frontmatter 'sources:' list are checked.
rules.fix: Add 'sources: [glob, ...]' to the frontmatter of docs that track specific code; refresh the doc when that code changes (tune config.doc_lag_days).

rules.title: The linter is actually enforced (run in CI or pre-commit)
rules.rationale: QUAL-01 checks a linter is configured; this checks it actually runs where it gates merges. A config nobody runs proves nothing. Skips if no linter is configured.
rules.fix: Invoke the linter in CI (or via 'pre-commit run --all-files') so a lint failure fails the build.

check.then_fail_detail: linter configured but never invoked in CI/pre-commit/Makefile

rules.title: CI grants a least-privilege GITHUB_TOKEN
rules.rationale: A workflow with no top-level permissions block (or write-all) hands every step a broad token; a compromised action or dependency can push code or publish packages. Default read-only, grant write per-job.
rules.fix: Add 'permissions: {contents: read}' (or 'read-all') at the top of each workflow; grant write scopes only inside the jobs that need them.

rules.title: A secret-scanning gate is wired in
rules.lesson: prevention gate vs point-in-time detection (complements SEC-01)
rules.rationale: SEC-01 catches secrets committed as of now; a scanner wired into CI or pre-commit fails future builds when a new secret lands.
rules.fix: Add gitleaks/trufflehog/detect-secrets to CI or .pre-commit-config.yaml (and commit its config).

rules.title: A dependency vulnerability scan runs in CI
rules.lesson: Scorecard Vulnerabilities (SCA is not an update-tool)
rules.rationale: An update bot (SEC-05: dependabot/renovate) opens PRs but is not a vuln scanner; a dedicated SCA step surfaces known CVEs in the pipeline.
rules.fix: Add an SCA step to CI: osv-scanner / govulncheck / pip-audit / npm audit / trivy / grype / snyk test / owasp dependency-check.

rules.title: Pre-commit hooks pinned to an immutable rev
rules.lesson: immutable rev, not a floating branch (analog of SEC-03)
rules.rationale: A pre-commit hook pinned to main/master/HEAD pulls whatever that branch points at — the same mutable-ref supply-chain risk SEC-03 guards for CI actions. Skips if there's no pre-commit config.
rules.fix: Pin each 'rev:' in .pre-commit-config.yaml to a tag or 40-char SHA, never a branch name.

rules.title: Repo descriptor present and valid
rules.lesson: C39 — the repo declares itself; applicability, scrutiny, and ownership consume a schema-validated descriptor, not per-rule tree-sniffing that misfires and can't express intent
rules.rationale: type/lifecycle/maturity/owner/workflow cannot be reliably inferred from the tree. A minimal baseline.repo.json is the one stored intent every derivation hangs off. Absent, the checker falls back to fragile auto-detection; present-but-invalid, its declared intent can't be trusted. Transitional WARN — a repo can adopt incrementally.
rules.fix: Add baseline.repo.json at the repo root: copy templates/baseline.repo.json or a config-presets/*.repo.json posture preset, then set type, lifecycle, maturity, owner, workflow, and anchoring. `init` scaffolds it as its first act.

rules.title: No hand-maintained status stamp (derive it instead)
rules.source: V2 ADR-0009 — a stored status stamp manufactures the multi-lane collision class it exists to prevent; state is derived by `baseline orient`, not hand-stamped
rules.lesson: CTX-12 stored-status tripwire — flags the artifact V2 retires (CTX-01) for migration
rules.rationale: A `last-verified:` stamp is a hand-maintained proxy for derived state (the drift-and-collide artifact of ADR-0009). Warn now while CTX-01 still gates the stamp; promotes to blocker once the migration to derived `orient` completes (M7). A repo with no status file simply skips.
rules.fix: Delete the status stamp and run `baseline orient` for derived state; the stamp's job moves to the tree/history/forge derivation.
